name: Urea
mainFunction: Fast nitrogen source for vegetative growth and leaf development.
bestUseCase: Top-dressing nitrogen in active growth when leaves are light green and crop has moisture.
stage: Never basal in puddled water; split by crop stage (tillering/branching/pre-flowering).
doseGuidance: Paddy: 3 splits (basal through other source + active tillering + panicle initiation). Wheat: 1/2 at sowing + 1/4 at CRI + 1/4 at first node. Tomato/Chilli: small split doses every 12-15 days, stop heavy N after first fruit set.
method: Apply on moist soil, not on dry cracked soil or standing water; irrigate lightly within 24 hours.
commonMistakes: Single heavy dose, applying before heavy rain, using only urea without potash, and late sprays that give leafy growth but low grain/fruit.
safety: Wear gloves and mask while handling; store in dry place and keep away from seed and children.
organicAlternative: Well decomposed FYM + vermicompost + Azospirillum/Azotobacter with green manuring

name: DAP
mainFunction: Strong starter fertilizer for root growth and early crop establishment.
bestUseCase: Basal use at sowing/transplanting in phosphorus-deficient fields or cool soils.
stage: Mostly basal; avoid late heavy use after flowering stage.
doseGuidance: Place 3-5 cm beside and below seed/seedling line. In paddy nursery/main field, use as basal and reduce if soil test P is high. In vegetables, combine with potash and calcium sources.
method: Band placement in root zone; avoid direct seed contact and avoid open broadcast in flooded water.
commonMistakes: Using DAP as the only fertilizer, overusing in high-P soils, and forgetting sulfur/potash which causes weak stem and poor grain/fruit filling.
safety: Use mask in dusty handling and wash hands after application.
organicAlternative: Rock phosphate or bone meal with PSB culture in acidic to neutral soils

name: MOP (Muriate of Potash)
mainFunction: Improves stress tolerance, grain filling, fruit quality and disease tolerance.
bestUseCase: Potash correction for grain filling, fruit size, drought tolerance, and stalk strength.
stage: Apply 50-70% basal and rest before reproductive stage (PI in paddy, booting in wheat, flowering/fruiting in vegetables).
doseGuidance: Paddy: split with basal + panicle initiation. Wheat: full basal in low-K soils or split with first irrigation. Tomato/Chilli: split from 20 DAT to fruit development. Mango: apply around drip line in 2 splits (post-harvest and pre-flowering).
method: Soil placement near root zone or through fertigation-compatible grade; mix lightly into soil.
commonMistakes: Skipping MOP when urea prices are high, applying too close to young roots, and using high chloride source in chloride-sensitive stages without checking crop need.
safety: Use gloves, avoid inhalation of dust, and keep bags away from moisture.
organicAlternative: Wood ash (tested quality) + banana pseudostem compost + tank silt compost

name: Zinc Sulphate
mainFunction: Corrects zinc deficiency and supports enzyme activity and tillering.
bestUseCase: Use only when deficiency signs or soil test indicates low Zn.
stage: Basal soil application or foliar correction
doseGuidance: Soil or foliar dose as per crop recommendation and label.
method: Soil mix or foliar spray after dilution.
safety: Do not mix randomly with incompatible products.

name: NPK 19:19:19
mainFunction: Balanced NPK support for nursery, vegetative growth, and recovery after stress.
bestUseCase: As foliar/fertigation support where quick balanced nutrition is needed.
stage: Nursery to pre-flowering; reduce once heavy fruit load starts and switch to higher K grades.
doseGuidance: Foliar: low concentration as per label (commonly 0.5-1%). Fertigation: small frequent doses based on crop stage and irrigation volume. Use only as supplement, not full nutrient plan.
method: Dissolve fully in clean water, spray in cool hours, and maintain pH-compatible tank mix.
commonMistakes: High concentration spray at noon, mixing with incompatible pesticides, and replacing full basal fertilizer with only water-soluble NPK.
safety: Do jar test before tank mix and avoid spraying in high heat/wind.
organicAlternative: Compost tea + fish amino/liquid bio-inputs with soil-applied compost

name: Azospirillum Biofertilizer
mainFunction: Supports biological N fixation and root growth.
bestUseCase: Seed/seedling treatment in cereals and millets.
doseGuidance: Use fresh culture and recommended quantity per seed/acre.
commonMistakes: Using expired culture or mixing with fungicide directly.

name: Vermicompost
mainFunction: Improves soil structure, microbial life, and moisture retention.
bestUseCase: Base soil health improvement before planting.
doseGuidance: Apply as per crop and soil organic carbon status.
safety: Use pathogen-free material from trusted source.

name: Imidacloprid
mainFunction: Controls sucking pests like aphids, jassids, and whiteflies.
bestUseCase: Early control of aphid/whitefly/jassid when pest count crosses threshold, especially nursery and young crop stage.
stage: Seed treatment, seedling dip, or early foliar use before severe infestation (as per crop label).
doseGuidance: Use exact crop-label dose and rotate with different mode of action after one spray cycle. Keep pre-harvest interval strictly.
method: Uniform spray on leaf underside in morning/evening; avoid repeated back-to-back sprays.
commonMistakes: Prophylactic weekly sprays, mixing many insecticides together, and spraying during peak bee activity.
safety: Highly toxic to pollinators; never spray on open flowers. Wear gloves, mask, and avoid drift to water bodies.
organicAlternative: Neem oil/azadirachtin + yellow sticky traps + border trap crops

name: Spinosad
mainFunction: Controls thrips and caterpillars in vegetables.
bestUseCase: When ETL is crossed for thrips or fruit borer.
doseGuidance: Follow label and rotate MoA to avoid resistance.
method: Uniform foliar spray with good underside coverage.
safety: Avoid drift to nearby water bodies.

name: Mancozeb
mainFunction: Broad protective control of leaf spots and blights.
bestUseCase: Preventive sprays in humid disease-favorable periods.
doseGuidance: Apply at recommended interval and concentration.
method: Foliar spray with complete canopy coverage.
commonMistakes: Late use after severe disease establishment.

name: Tricyclazole
mainFunction: Targeted management of rice blast disease.
bestUseCase: Early blast symptoms or high-risk weather window.
doseGuidance: Apply only as labeled in paddy and avoid unnecessary repeats.
method: Foliar spray with interval as advised.
safety: Follow PPE and residue waiting period.

name: Pendimethalin
bestUseCase: Within 1-3 days after sowing/transplanting as recommended.
doseGuidance: Use crop-specific label dose with proper soil moisture.
commonMistakes: Spraying on dry cloddy soil or using post-emergence.
safety: Avoid overlap spray to prevent crop injury.

name: Glyphosate
mainFunction: Controls existing broad-spectrum weeds in non-crop zone.
stage: Before planting or directed non-crop spray
doseGuidance: Never spray over standing crop unless label permits.
commonMistakes: Drift injury to crop and nearby plants.
safety: Use strict PPE and avoid windy conditions.

name: Neem Oil
mainFunction: Repels/suppresses sucking pests and soft-bodied insects.
bestUseCase: Early-stage low to moderate pest pressure in IPM.
doseGuidance: Use correct dilution and repeat as per label.
method: Foliar spray with emulsifier as required.
commonMistakes: Using low-quality product or wrong concentration.
safety: Safer option but still avoid direct contact and inhalation.

name: Chlorpyrifos
mainFunction: Controls soil and foliage pests like termites, cutworms, stem borers (only where legally permitted).
bestUseCase: Spot use for severe soil insect attack after confirming safer options are not sufficient.
stage: Pre-sowing soil treatment or early vegetative stage as directed application.
doseGuidance: Follow current state registration and label exactly; avoid use near harvest and never exceed waiting period.
method: Directed spray or soil drench with PPE; avoid blanket spray in the entire field.
commonMistakes: Using near harvest, high dose mixing with pyrethroids, and spraying without pest scouting.
safety: Toxic to humans, fish, and beneficial insects. Full PPE, no entry to field until spray deposit dries, keep livestock away.
organicAlternative: Neem cake in soil + Trichoderma + deep summer ploughing and field sanitation

name: Cypermethrin
mainFunction: Fast knockdown control of caterpillars and some chewing insects.
bestUseCase: Emergency knockdown when larval population is above ETL and crop is before harvest safety window.
stage: Vegetative to early reproductive stage depending on pest incidence.
doseGuidance: Use minimum effective label dose; rotate with non-pyrethroid molecules to avoid resistance and resurgence.
method: Even canopy spray in evening; ensure nozzle calibration and avoid drift.
commonMistakes: Frequent repeated use leading to resistance, killing natural enemies, and secondary pest outbreak (mites/aphids).
safety: Wear PPE, avoid spraying during windy hours, and follow re-entry and pre-harvest interval.
alternatives: Bt formulations for early larval stage
organicAlternative: NSKE 5% + pheromone traps + hand picking in early infestation
